title: leontodon collinus
intro: Leontodon collinus, also known as the Autumn Hawk's-bit, is a perennial plant species in the dandelion family that grows in a rosette of leaves and produces small yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can be attributed to its high content of sesquiterpene lactones. The health benefits of Leontodon collinus include its ability to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory problems. Leontodon collinus is typically prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
